prompt_text: Checkly vs PagerDuty pricing and features
response:
Checkly and PagerDuty solve different problems—Checkly is a **synthetic monitoring** tool, while PagerDuty is an **incident management/on‑call** platform—so they are more complementary than direct substitutes. For pricing, Checkly is generally cheaper and resource‑based, while PagerDuty is per‑user and can become expensive as teams grow.[1][4][7]

Below is a focused comparison of **pricing and core features** so you can decide which (or both) fits your use case.

---

## 1. Pricing comparison

### Checkly pricing (synthetic monitoring)

According to Checkly’s pricing and recent comparison data:[1][7]

- **Hobby – Free**
  - **$0/month**
  - ~10 monitors  
  - ~1,000 browser checks / month  
  - ~10,000 API checks / month  
  - ~4 locations  

- **Starter – $29/month**
  - ~50 monitors  
  - ~3,000 browser checks / month  
  - ~25,000 API checks / month  
  - 4 locations  
  - Up to 1‑minute check frequency[1]  

- **Team – $80/month**
  - ~75 monitors  
  - ~12,000 browser checks / month  
  - ~100,000 API checks / month  
  - All ~22 global locations  
  - Up to 30‑second check frequency[1]  

- **Enterprise – Custom**
  - Unlimited monitors  
  - Check frequency down to 1 second  
  - Dedicated support, advanced security, private locations, etc.[1]  

Overall, **pricing scales with number of checks/monitors and frequency**, not number of users.

---

### PagerDuty pricing (incident management & on‑call)

PagerDuty is charged **per user per month** with extra cost for add‑ons, according to official pricing and independent analyses.[1][3][4]

From PagerDuty’s incident management pricing:[4]

- **Free**
  - **$0/month** for up to 5 users  
  - 1 on‑call schedule, 1 escalation policy  
  - 100 international phone/SMS notifications per month  
  - 750+ integrations  
  - Basic incident response[4]  

- **Professional**
  - **$25/user/month list**; billed annually, often **$21/user/month effective**[3][4]  
  - Everything in Free, plus:  
    - More notifications, more schedules/policies  
    - Basic Slack/Teams chat integration  
    - External status page (up to 250 subscribers)  
    - SSO, basic ticketing integrations  
    - Limited major incident workflow template, post‑incident reviews, email support[1][4]  

- **Business**
  - **~$49/user/month** (official page)[1][4]  
  - Adds:  
    - Custom fields, advanced ITSM integrations  
    - Internal status pages  
    - Multi‑year data access  
    - More PagerDuty Advance credits (automation)[1]  

- **Enterprise**
  - **Custom pricing**; typical large accounts range roughly **$5,000–$70,000/year total**, depending on users and add‑ons.[2]  
  - Adds:  
    - Advanced incident workflows (conditionals, loops, delays)  
    - Premium Slack actions, up to 100 custom incident types  
    - Bi‑directional ServiceNow sync, premium status pages, stakeholder licenses, live call routing, etc.[1][4]  

Many capabilities (advanced analytics, AIOps, status pages, etc.) can be **paid add‑ons**, making PagerDuty significantly more expensive as you add features and users.[2][3][5][6]

---

### High‑level pricing pattern

| Aspect                        | **Checkly**                                           | **PagerDuty**                                                    |
|------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Pricing model                | By **monitors/check volume**                          | By **user** (plus **add‑ons**)                                   |
| Entry paid tier              | **$29/month** (Starter)[1]                            | **~$21–25/user/month** (Professional)[3][4]                      |
| Free plan                    | Hobby, small usage                                    | Free, up to 5 users, limited capabilities[4]                     |
| Typical cost driver          | How many checks & how often                           | How many users, and which advanced features/add‑ons[2][3][6]     |
| Cost scaling for large orgs  | Custom enterprise, but still check‑based              | Can reach **tens of thousands/year** at scale[2]                 |

---

## 2. Feature comparison

Because they target different parts of the reliability stack, the core features differ significantly.

### What Checkly focuses on
